thesis: Deep value (top-decile cheapness) confirmed by strengthening revisions and non-negative momentum; oil/gas re-rating leg with a defensive dividend floor suits a multi-asset book.
falsification: Crude rolls over and analyst revisions turn negative, or ONGC underperforms the broad energy basket over the next quarter despite its cheapness.

thesis: Refining-margin cyclical trading at a deep discount where revisions are firming — a mean-reversion bet with analyst support, sized modestly as a mid-cap.
falsification: Refining cracks compress and revisions roll over, or the stock keeps lagging while peers (ONGC) re-rate — signalling a structural, not cyclical, discount.

thesis: Shipping-cycle cyclical where price momentum, valuation and analyst revisions agree — highest signal-confluence name in the universe, so I lean in.
falsification: Freight rates/shipping cycle peak and momentum breaks down, or revisions fade — the confluence that justified the overweight disappears.

thesis: Metals cyclical with firming analyst revisions and positive relative momentum; a liquid large-cap way to add commodity beta to the book.
falsification: Iron-ore prices and steel demand weaken, momentum turns negative, and revisions reverse lower.

thesis: Aluminium cyclical with the best momentum among the metals names plus strengthening revisions — a leveraged play on the base-metals up-leg.
falsification: LME aluminium rolls over, momentum breaks, and revisions stall — the commodity tailwind reverses.

thesis: Diversified metals (aluminium + Novelis) with cheapness, positive momentum and improving revisions; a core liquid cyclical holding.
falsification: Global aluminium spreads and Novelis margins deteriorate, momentum fades, and revisions turn lower.

thesis: High-yield energy cyclical where revisions are firming and momentum is positive; income plus optionality on power demand, low-beta ballast.
falsification: Coal volumes/realizations disappoint, the dividend is cut, and revisions roll over.

thesis: Dominant life insurer with strongly rising analyst revisions and reasonable valuation; a structural franchise where the Street is upgrading.
falsification: Revisions stall, VNB/margin disappoints, and the stock continues to de-rate versus private life insurers.

thesis: Gold-finance franchise riding a high gold-price backdrop with firming analyst revisions — a structural financial with a commodity tailwind.
falsification: Gold prices fall sharply or regulatory tightening on gold loans hits AUM/yields, and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Cheap private bank with stabilizing-to-rising analyst revisions; a turnaround/re-rating play within the financials sleeve.
falsification: Asset-quality or microfinance stress re-emerges, revisions reverse, and the discount to peers widens further.

thesis: Ferrous-metals cyclical with positive momentum and cheapness; a tactical add on the steel up-cycle and infra demand.
falsification: Steel prices/spreads fall, momentum breaks, and revisions soften — the cyclical leg ends.

thesis: Conglomerate with strengthening revisions and exposure to multiple cyclical/structural legs (cement, fibre, new-paints) at fair value.
falsification: Paints-foray cash burn and cement oversupply weigh on returns and revisions turn lower.

thesis: Sterile-injectables franchise where analyst revisions are strongly positive and momentum is improving; structural pharma exposure.
falsification: US injectables pricing pressure or plant/regulatory issues hit revisions and momentum reverses.

thesis: Diversified pharma with firming revisions and stable momentum; defensive growth that lowers book correlation to cyclicals.
falsification: US generics base erodes faster than expected, key approvals slip, and revisions turn negative.

thesis: IT-services compounder with strongly rising revisions; the price weakness is the entry, revisions signal order-book strength.
falsification: Deal momentum stalls, revisions reverse, and the stock continues to underperform the IT index.

thesis: Regulated-return power utility with a capex/green pivot; low-beta income that anchors the multi-asset book.
falsification: Regulatory returns are cut or green-capex execution slips, and revisions/momentum fade.

thesis: Forgings franchise with improving revisions, positive momentum and diversification into defence/non-auto — a cyclical-with-structural-kicker.
falsification: Global CV/auto demand weakens, defence ramp disappoints, momentum breaks and revisions reverse.

thesis: High-quality industrial with a defence/mining order-book tailwind, strong revisions and positive momentum — structural compounding.
falsification: Defence order inflow slows, margins compress, and revisions/momentum roll over; rich valuation de-rates.

thesis: Base/precious-metals cyclical with positive momentum, improving revisions and a strong dividend — commodity ballast with income.
falsification: Zinc/silver prices fall, the dividend is cut, momentum breaks and revisions turn lower.

thesis: Two-wheeler/exports franchise with improving revisions, pricing power and a robust balance sheet; lower-beta consumer-cyclical.
falsification: Domestic 2W demand and export markets weaken together, margins fall, and revisions reverse.

thesis: SUV + tractor leader with structural product cycle, but current revisions and momentum are soft, so I hold a reduced position.
falsification: SUV order book or tractor cycle rolls over and revisions deteriorate further.

thesis: Highest-quality large private bank with steady franchise compounding; the spine of the financials sleeve in a lower-beta book.
falsification: NIMs compress sharply, credit costs rise, and the bank loses share to peers while revisions turn negative.

thesis: Dominant deposit franchise post-merger normalizing; a low-volatility financial anchor rather than a high-conviction overweight.
falsification: Deposit-growth and NIM normalization stall, revisions keep falling, and it underperforms ICICI persistently.

thesis: Largest PSU bank at undemanding valuation with a steady credit cycle; a value-financial anchor, sized modestly given soft revisions.
falsification: Asset quality deteriorates in the corporate/SME book and revisions roll over while the valuation discount fails to close.

thesis: Improving private bank franchise at a fair multiple; diversifies the financial sleeve beyond ICICI/HDFC.
falsification: Loan growth slows, credit costs surprise, and revisions turn negative versus larger peers.

thesis: High-quality conservative private bank; current revisions/momentum are soft so I hold a reduced weight rather than exit.
falsification: Growth continues to lag peers and revisions deteriorate while the premium valuation persists.

thesis: Best-in-class consumer-NBFC compounder held at a reduced weight pending a turn in momentum/revisions.
falsification: AUM growth or asset quality disappoints and revisions keep falling while the premium multiple compresses.

thesis: Vehicle-finance NBFC at a low multiple; held small because revisions are currently negative despite the cheap valuation.
falsification: CV cycle weakens, credit costs rise, and revisions stay negative — the value discount proves justified.

thesis: Tier-1 IT services franchise providing low-beta, cash-generative defensiveness; a market-weight anchor in a multi-asset book.
falsification: Discretionary tech spend stays weak, revisions fall further, and the stock underperforms the IT index.

thesis: Tier-1 IT leader with the steadiest revisions in the group; defensive cash-return compounder for the lower-beta sleeve.
falsification: Global IT demand deteriorates, deal TCV slows, and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Margin-recovery story held at a reduced weight; I prefer TCS/COFORGE for cleaner revisions.
falsification: Margin recovery stalls and revisions fade while peers outperform.

thesis: Mid-tier IT held small; momentum and revisions do not justify a full weight versus stronger IT names.
falsification: Order book and revisions keep deteriorating and the stock lags the IT index.

thesis: Wireless/Africa/digital franchise with ARPU-led earnings growth; a structural, lower-correlation holding.
falsification: Tariff hikes stall, competitive intensity rises, and revisions/momentum turn down together.

thesis: Cash-generative staples + cigarettes with a high dividend; defensive ballast, sized down on soft current signals.
falsification: Cigarette taxation tightens or FMCG margins compress and revisions keep falling.

thesis: Top FMCG franchise held as low-beta ballast; sized down because momentum and revisions are currently weak.
falsification: Rural recovery and volume growth disappoint and revisions stay negative.

thesis: High-quality packaged-foods franchise where revisions are improving; low-beta defensive growth.
falsification: Input-cost inflation or volume slowdown hits margins and revisions reverse lower.

thesis: Value-retail franchise with long runway; held at a reduced weight pending a turn in revisions/momentum.
falsification: Like-for-like growth slows, online competition bites, and revisions keep falling.

thesis: Bottling/distribution growth story held smaller after a strong run; signals are now only mildly supportive.
falsification: Volume growth decelerates, new-geography ramp disappoints, and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Energy-efficiency/clean-tech capital-goods play on the industrial capex cycle with positive momentum.
falsification: Order inflow slows, execution margins compress, and momentum/revisions fade.

thesis: Leading hospital network with structural healthcare demand; held smaller while revisions are soft.
falsification: Occupancy/ARPOB growth slows, digital cash-burn drags, and revisions stay weak.

thesis: Best-in-class general insurer providing low-correlation financial exposure; sized down on soft current signals.
falsification: Combined ratio worsens and revisions keep falling while the stock de-rates.

thesis: Conglomerate with multiple value-unlock legs (Jio, retail, new energy) at undemanding valuation; a core lower-beta holding.
falsification: O2C margins compress, new-energy capex disappoints, and revisions keep falling while the stock de-rates.

thesis: Ferrous cyclical with positive price momentum; sized down because revisions are soft, complementing SAIL/NMDC.
falsification: Steel spreads and Europe operations deteriorate, momentum breaks, and revisions stay negative.
